dimensions.name: Cyclomatic Complexity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How tangled the control flow is — methods with many branches are hard to test and change.

dimensions.name: Cognitive Complexity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How hard the code is for a person to follow, beyond raw branching.

dimensions.name: God Classes
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Over-large classes that try to do too much ("god classes").

dimensions.name: Code Duplication
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Copy-pasted code that should be shared instead.

dimensions.name: Coupling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether volatile projects sit underneath others that depend on them (so their churn ripples upward), and whether project dependencies form cycles. A widely-depended-on but stable shared/kernel project is healthy, not penalised.

dimensions.name: Cohesion (LCOM4)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether a class's methods are focused on a single responsibility.

dimensions.name: Architectural Integrity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the code respects its intended layering / architecture rules.

dimensions.name: Code Coverage
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How much of the code is actually exercised by tests.

dimensions.name: Test Distribution
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the test suite has a healthy mix of unit / integration / end-to-end tests.

dimensions.name: Test Quality
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the tests truly assert behaviour rather than just running the code.

dimensions.name: Test Reliability
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the tests pass reliably, with no flakiness.

dimensions.name: Dependency Hygiene
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether dependencies are current, secure, and not bloated.

dimensions.name: Secret Scanning
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether any secrets (keys, tokens, passwords) have leaked into the code.

dimensions.name: License Compliance
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the licenses of third-party packages are compatible with your policy.

dimensions.name: Churn × Complexity Hotspots
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Files that change often and are also complex — the riskiest hotspots.

dimensions.name: Bus Factor
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether knowledge is concentrated in too few people (the "bus factor").

dimensions.name: Explicit Debt
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Acknowledged debt left in the code — TODOs, dead code, suppressed warnings.

dimensions.name: Solution Shape
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the solution is laid out in a sensible, conventional structure.

dimensions.name: Documentation Quality
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the project's documentation is clear, complete, and useful.

dimensions.name: ADR Quality
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether architecture decisions are recorded well (context, decision, consequences).

dimensions.name: Naming Consistency
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether names — types, methods, variables — are clear and consistent.

dimensions.name: Internal API Consistency
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the internal API surface is consistent and coherent.

dimensions.name: Boundary Type-Coupling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether domain types (IDs, enums, value objects) leak across bounded-context boundaries.

dimensions.name: Comment Value
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether comments are worth it — explaining WHY (valuable) rather than WHAT (redundant).

dimensions.name: ADR Conformance
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the code actually follows the decisions recorded in the project's ADRs.

dimensions.name: Project Cohesion
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether each project is a focused, coherent unit rather than an oversized grab-bag.

dimensions.name: Navigability
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How far you must trace to follow a call — low indirection and co-located slices read easier.

dimensions.name: Secrets (history)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether any secrets were ever committed — scanned across the full git history, not just now.

dimensions.name: Static Analysis (SAST)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Real static-analysis (SAST) findings — likely security bugs in the code, any language.

dimensions.name: Dependency Vulnerabilities
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether any dependencies have known published vulnerabilities (CVEs), direct or transitive.

dimensions.name: IaC & Container Security
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether Dockerfiles / Terraform / Kubernetes config follow security best practices.

dimensions.name: Data Compliance (PII/GDPR)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Likely personal-data (PII / GDPR) handling concerns — logging or storing data without safeguards.

dimensions.name: JS/npm Dependency Vulnerabilities
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether JavaScript/npm dependencies have known published vulnerabilities (CVEs) — the npm ecosystem's biggest risk.

dimensions.name: Knowledge Freshness
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether anyone still has living knowledge of each file, or it has been orphaned — last understood long ago by someone now gone quiet. The sibling of the bus factor: D16 asks who owns it, D34 asks whether anyone still knows it.

dimensions.name: Change Coupling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether files that change together actually belong together — pairs that repeatedly co-change in git history despite having no explicit code dependency, surfacing the hidden/logical coupling (and boundaries in the wrong place) a static scan can't see.

dimensions.name: Supply-chain Provenance & Signing
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the build pipeline provides supply-chain integrity — generated provenance/attestation, signed artifacts (cosign/sigstore), an SBOM, and pinned build actions. Presence of the configuration, not a runtime guarantee.

dimensions.name: Vulnerability-disclosure Policy
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the repository publishes a coordinated-vulnerability-disclosure policy (SECURITY.md or security.txt) with a reporting contact, so finders know how to report a vulnerability. Presence of a policy file with a contact, not whether the policy is adequate or honoured.

dimensions.name: OSV Dependency Vulnerabilities
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether dependencies have known published vulnerabilities (CVEs) per the OSV database — npm and other lockfile ecosystems, parsed natively. Complements D33 (npm via trivy) and D30 (.NET via dotnet).

dimensions.name: Text alternatives
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether non-text content carries a text alternative — img/area/input[type=image] have alt, a meaningful svg has a title or aria-label, and video has a captions track. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: Forms & labels
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether form controls have a programmatic label (an associated label, aria-label or aria-labelledby), buttons have text, fieldsets have a legend, and a placeholder isn't used as the only label. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: Page structure
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether pages declare a language and title, expose a main landmark and a sane heading order, keep zoom enabled, title their iframes and avoid meta-refresh. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: Keyboard semantics
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether interactive behaviour is keyboard-reachable — no click handler on a non-interactive element lacking a role, tabindex and key handler, no positive tabindex, no href-less anchor. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: ARIA correctness
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether ARIA is used correctly — valid non-abstract roles, the ARIA state a role requires, and no aria-hidden on a focusable element. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: Visual & motion safety
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether focus outlines aren't removed without a replacement, motion respects prefers-reduced-motion, and literal CSS colour pairs meet contrast — PARTIAL: only inline styles and in-repo CSS literals are visible, never computed or runtime colour. Static markup readiness, not a WCAG conformance claim.

dimensions.name: A11y enforcement
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether accessibility is ENFORCED in the toolchain — an a11y linter (eslint-plugin-jsx-a11y / vuejs-accessibility) configured, and axe/pa11y/Lighthouse wired into tests or CI — on the Documented→Verified→Prevented ladder.

dimensions.name: Captive dependencies
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether any singleton service captures a scoped/transient dependency — a silent lifetime/threading bug.

dimensions.name: Stateful singletons
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether singleton services avoid mutable shared instance state that concurrent callers would race on.

dimensions.name: Project dependency cycles
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the project-reference graph is acyclic (cycles block independent build/deploy and signal eroding boundaries).

dimensions.name: Dependency direction
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether dependencies point inward (Domain ← Application ← Infrastructure/Web) — the clean-architecture dependency rule, checked across the project graph.

dimensions.name: Architecture & structure
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the codebase has a recognisable, scale-appropriate structure (a named architectural style, or modular enough for its size) rather than being an ad-hoc ball of mud.

dimensions.name: Interface segregation
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether interfaces stay focused rather than fat — the Interface-Segregation principle (SOLID 'I').

dimensions.name: Slice cohesion
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether feature slices stay independent (no direct cross-slice references) — the discipline that makes vertical-slice architecture pay off.

dimensions.name: Test isolation
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether production projects stay free of references to test projects — tests may depend on production, never the reverse.

dimensions.name: CQS / query purity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether read (query) handlers stay side-effect-free — a query that writes persistent state or raises events breaks CQS and makes reads unsafe to retry, cache, or route to a read replica.

dimensions.name: Code composition
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How the codebase splits by code ROLE — domain, application, infrastructure, test, generated. The significance map behind the knowledge/coupling weighting, and a DDD signal in its own right: a thin domain core under fat infrastructure is the anemic-domain smell, quantified.

dimensions.name: Data Protection
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether sensitive data is encrypted at rest and in transit and keys are vaulted.

dimensions.name: Access Controls
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether access is authorized by default — [Authorize]/policies or imperative guard methods (throw-on-violation) called from handlers.

dimensions.name: Audit Trail
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether changes to sensitive data are recorded (who, what, when) for compliance + incident response.

dimensions.name: Data Retention
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether data has a defined lifetime — retention periods, TTLs, cleanup jobs (storage limitation).

dimensions.name: Data-Subject Rights
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether GDPR data-subject requests are supported in code — erasure, export/portability, consent.

dimensions.name: Aggregate boundaries
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether aggregates reference each other by identity (id) rather than by direct object reference — the core DDD consistency-boundary rule.

dimensions.name: Strongly-typed ids
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How much of the domain uses strongly-typed ids vs raw Guid/string/int — adoption curve, not all-or-nothing.

dimensions.name: Integration-event coupling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether cross-context integration events stay loosely coupled — no producer-owned enums/domain types leaking to consumers. Shared-kernel/contracts types are allowed.

dimensions.name: Rich vs anemic model
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether aggregates/entities carry the behaviour that protects their invariants, rather than being data bags driven by external services.

dimensions.name: Encapsulated state
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether entities protect their state (private/init-only setters) instead of exposing public setters that bypass invariants. Softened when a rehydration framework (Marten/EF) is present.

dimensions.name: Domain ↔ infrastructure boundary
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the domain layer stays free of infrastructure dependencies (EF/Marten/HTTP/ASP.NET) — the clean-architecture dependency rule.

dimensions.name: Repository granularity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether repositories are per aggregate root (not per child entity) so the root's invariants can't be bypassed.

dimensions.name: Value-object opportunities
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether clusters of primitives that travel together (a missing value object) are extracted — a low-weight suggestion, LLM-confirmed when configured.

dimensions.name: Handler temporal coupling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether event handlers stay asynchronous (no blocking remote HTTP/gRPC calls awaited inside a handler).

dimensions.name: Event/command shape
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether commands have a single handler (one owner of the decision) and fan-out is modelled with events.

dimensions.name: Event naming
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether events are named in the past tense (a clarity nudge — low weight).

dimensions.name: Outbox / dual-write
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether state changes and message publishes are atomic (a transactional outbox) rather than a crash-unsafe dual write.

dimensions.name: Idempotency
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether retry-prone mutations (command handlers + message/event consumers) are idempotent so an at-least-once redelivery or client retry doesn't double-apply the effect — heuristic at-risk detection confirmed by language model, advisory.

dimensions.name: Fold determinism
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether Apply/When folds reconstruct state purely from the event (no DateTime.Now, Guid.NewGuid, Random or IO) so replay is reproducible.

dimensions.name: Immutable events
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether persisted events are immutable facts (init-only/readonly) — a settable event member lets stored history be rewritten.

dimensions.name: PII in the event store
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the append-only event log avoids un-erasable personal data (or has a crypto-shredding strategy) — a GDPR right-to-erasure risk.

dimensions.name: Unfinished & placeholder code
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Unreviewed-generation residue: shipped members still throwing NotImplementedException, and placeholder string literals left in non-test, non-generated code. Scored as a quality signature, never as a claim about authorship.

dimensions.name: Incompleteness & stubs
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Unfinished work detected by code SHAPE, not keywords: members that only throw a "not implemented" exception, methods that take inputs and return a constant, async methods that never await, dead `if (false)` / `#if false` branches, and skeleton types most of whose members are holes. A real, objective slice of technical debt.

dimensions.name: Personal-Data Handling (GDPR)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: LLM-confirmed personal-data handling surface — fields that are genuine personal data about a real person (not fiction/test/config), surfaced for the GDPR Art. 25/32 self-assessment. Advisory.

dimensions.name: Alt-Text Quality (WCAG 1.1.1)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: LLM-judged quality of image alt text — whether each alt is a meaningful equivalent rather than a filename, generic word or placeholder. Advisory; complements the deterministic 'alt present?' check.

dimensions.name: Vulnerability-Disclosure Policy
dimensions.whatItMeasures: LLM-judged whether a SECURITY.md / security.txt is a real coordinated vulnerability-disclosure policy (contact + how to report + handling), not just a stub. Advisory.

dimensions.name: Dev/Test/Prod Separation
dimensions.whatItMeasures: LLM-judged whether configuration separates development/test/production (distinct per-environment settings) rather than commingling them. Advisory.

dimensions.name: Documentation (README)
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the repo and its projects have a README, and whether it's substantive and current.

dimensions.name: Architecture documentation
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether key decisions (ADRs) and the high-level shape (C4/diagrams) are written down.

dimensions.name: Folder & project structure
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the repo is organised deliberately — src/test separation and consistent project naming.

dimensions.name: Documentation accuracy
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the README actually describes the code that exists (LLM-judged, advisory).

dimensions.name: CI/CD gates
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether an automated pipeline builds and tests every change.

dimensions.name: Observability
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the code is diagnosable in production — structured logging, tracing/metrics, health checks.

dimensions.name: Security & performance tooling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether SAST, secret/dependency scanning and performance benchmarking are wired in (presence, not runtime).

dimensions.name: Deployment & Rollback
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether releases are automated and safely reversible (probes, rolling updates, approval gates) — from manifests/pipeline files, not the live environment.

dimensions.name: DR & Backup
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether disaster recovery is planned and codified — backups, geo-recovery, RTO/RPO, persistence guarantees — from IaC + container manifests + docs, never the live cloud.

dimensions.name: Release Hygiene
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether releases are traceable — a maintained changelog and explicit version stamping.

dimensions.name: Outbound HTTP resilience
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether outbound HTTP calls are wrapped in resilience (retry/timeout/circuit-breaker) so a failing dependency doesn't cascade.

dimensions.name: Schema migrations
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether EF Core schema changes go through versioned migrations rather than the un-evolvable EnsureCreated().

dimensions.name: Domain vs controller coverage
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether test coverage concentrates on the domain (business rules) rather than the trivial web/controller layer — a focus check a generic tool can't make.

dimensions.name: Library API & versioning
dimensions.whatItMeasures: For a library: a deliberate (small) public API surface and explicit semantic versioning so consumers can depend on it safely.

dimensions.name: BDD / executable specs
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether behaviour is captured as executable Gherkin specifications (a plus for shared understanding) — only assessed when a BDD framework is present.

dimensions.name: CI test-gate honesty
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the CI gate executes the test inventory it appears to have: excluded suites, skipped tests, and coverage collected without a threshold.

dimensions.name: Benchmark discipline
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the library protects its performance with benchmarks — a BenchmarkDotNet suite, an allocation MemoryDiagnoser, and (ideally) a CI gate. Presence is credited as a bonus, never a deduction.

dimensions.name: Allocation hygiene
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the code is written to minimise allocations so it doesn't pressure its host's GC — Span/Memory, pooling (ArrayPool/ObjectPool), stackalloc, ValueTask, value-type structs and buffer writers. Reward-only: credited where present, never penalised where a simpler style is fine.

dimensions.name: Async & latency hygiene
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether asynchronous code keeps its host responsive — a library awaits with ConfigureAwait(false) (so it never captures and stalls the host's context) and avoids sync-over-async blocking (.Wait()/.GetAwaiter().GetResult()) that wastes threads and risks deadlock.

dimensions.name: Type Safety
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How much of the frontend is typed TypeScript vs untyped JavaScript.

dimensions.name: Cyclomatic Complexity
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Per-function cyclomatic/cognitive complexity from the token-level function scanner (D-386) — real branching, not a regex heuristic.

dimensions.name: Large Files
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How many components/modules exceed the large-file threshold.

dimensions.name: Test Coverage
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Static test reachability (D-386): the share of production files reachable from any test via the import graph — measured without running anything.

dimensions.name: Dependency Freshness
dimensions.whatItMeasures: How outdated the npm dependencies are (a maturity signal). JS/npm CVEs are scored separately in D33 (JS/npm Dependency Vulnerabilities).

dimensions.name: Tooling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the package.json wires up test, lint and typecheck scripts.

dimensions.name: Dead Code
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Files unreachable from every application/tooling/test entry point, and exports nothing imports (module-graph reachability, D-386).

dimensions.name: Dependency Hygiene
dimensions.whatItMeasures: npm dependency truthfulness (D-386): unused dependencies, imports not declared anywhere, and type-/test-only packages shipped as production deps.

dimensions.name: Circular Imports
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Import cycles in the module graph (D-386) — files that can only be understood and changed together.

dimensions.name: Code Duplication
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Copy-pasted token-identical blocks across the frontend (the D4 clone algorithm over JS/TS tokens, D-386).

dimensions.name: Import Boundaries
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Conformance to the detected frontend architecture layout (feature-sliced / layered src) plus cross-package deep-import rules (D-386).

dimensions.name: Web-Security Posture
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Transport security, security headers, secure cookies, input validation, middleware order and crypto hygiene (presence, not runtime).

dimensions.name: Async correctness
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether the code avoids sync-over-async (deadlock-prone blocking on tasks) and async void.

dimensions.name: Cancellation propagation
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether async methods accept a CancellationToken so work can be cancelled (adoption curve).

dimensions.name: Exception handling
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether exceptions are handled rather than silently swallowed or rethrown with lost stack traces.

dimensions.name: Structured logging
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether log calls use message templates (queryable) rather than interpolated strings.

dimensions.name: Nullable reference types
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether nullable reference types are enabled and not undermined by heavy `!` suppression.

dimensions.name: Hand-rolled structured-format parsing
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether JSON/XML is parsed with regex/string hacks while a real parser is already referenced in the same project — a classic source of silent data bugs.

dimensions.name: Silent fallback defaults
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether parse/lookup failure paths return hard-coded constants with no logging or throw — failures that convert data errors into silent behavior changes.

dimensions.name: JS interop contract
dimensions.whatItMeasures: Whether string-named JS interop bindings resolve against the first-party JavaScript, and whether anything guards the boundary against renames.
